optional_params.name: columns
optional_params.description: List of column names to use as features. If None, all numeric columns will be used (optional)

optional_params.name: id_column
optional_params.description: Column containing IDs to exclude from features (optional)

optional_params.name: handle_missing
optional_params.description: Whether to handle missing values (default: True)

outputs.description: Function that extracts features from a SegmentationPipe.

get_features.description:
Extract features from dataframe for segmentation

Parameters:
-----------
columns : List[str], optional
    List of column names to use as features
    If None, all numeric columns will be used
id_column : str, optional
    Column containing IDs to exclude from features
handle_missing : bool, default=True
    Whether to handle missing values
scale_features : bool, default=True
    Whether to scale features

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that extracts features from a SegmentationPipe

optional_params.name: max_clusters
optional_params.description: Maximum number of clusters to try if find_optimal=True (default: 10)

optional_params.name: find_optimal
optional_params.description: Whether to find the optimal number of clusters using silhouette score (default: False)

optional_params.name: segment_name
optional_params.description: Name to use for this segmentation (default: 'kmeans')

outputs.description: Function that performs KMeans segmentation in a SegmentationPipe.

run_kmeans.description:
Run KMeans clustering for segmentation

Parameters:
-----------
n_clusters : int, default=5
    Number of clusters
max_clusters : int, default=10
    Maximum number of clusters to try if find_optimal=True
find_optimal : bool, default=False
    Whether to find the optimal number of clusters using silhouette score
random_state : int, default=42
    Random state for reproducibility
segment_name : str, default='kmeans'
    Name to use for this segmentation

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that performs KMeans segmentation in a SegmentationPipe

optional_params.name: eps
optional_params.description: The maximum distance between two samples for one to be considered as neighbors (default: 0.5)

optional_params.name: min_samples
optional_params.description: The number of samples in a neighborhood for a point to be considered a core point (default: 5)

optional_params.name: segment_name
optional_params.description: Name to use for this segmentation (default: 'dbscan')

outputs.description: Function that performs DBSCAN segmentation in a SegmentationPipe.

run_dbscan.description:
Run DBSCAN clustering for segmentation

Parameters:
-----------
eps : float, default=0.5
    The maximum distance between two samples for one to be considered as neighbors
min_samples : int, default=5
    The number of samples in a neighborhood for a point to be considered a core point
segment_name : str, default='dbscan'
    Name to use for this segmentation

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that performs DBSCAN segmentation in a SegmentationPipe

optional_params.name: segment_name
optional_params.description: Name to use for this segmentation (default: 'hierarchical')

outputs.description: Function that performs hierarchical segmentation in a SegmentationPipe.

run_hierarchical.description:
Run Hierarchical (Agglomerative) clustering for segmentation

Parameters:
-----------
n_clusters : int, default=5
    Number of clusters
linkage : str, default='ward'
    Linkage method ('ward', 'complete', 'average', 'single')
segment_name : str, default='hierarchical'
    Name to use for this segmentation

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that performs hierarchical segmentation in a SegmentationPipe

required_params.name: rules
required_params.description: Dictionary with segment names as keys and rule conditions as values. Example: {'high_value': {'spending': ('>', 100), 'frequency': ('>', 10)}, 'medium_value': {'spending': ('between', 50, 100)}}

optional_params.name: segment_name
optional_params.description: Name to use for this segmentation (default: 'rule_based')

outputs.description: Function that performs rule-based segmentation in a SegmentationPipe.

run_rule_based.description:
Run rule-based segmentation

Parameters:
-----------
rules : Dict[str, Dict]
    Dictionary with segment names as keys and rule conditions as values
    Example: {
        'high_value': {'spending': ('>', 100), 'frequency': ('>', 10)},
        'medium_value': {'spending': ('between', 50, 100)}
    }
segment_name : str, default='rule_based'
    Name to use for this segmentation

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that performs rule-based segmentation in a SegmentationPipe

optional_params.name: algorithm
optional_params.description: Name of the segmentation algorithm to summarize. If None, uses the current analysis (optional)

outputs.description: Function that generates summary statistics in a SegmentationPipe.

generate_summary.description:
Generate summary statistics for segments

Parameters:
-----------
algorithm : str, optional
    Name of the segmentation algorithm to summarize
    If None, uses the current analysis

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that generates summary statistics in a SegmentationPipe

required_params.name: segment_id
required_params.description: ID of the segment to extract

optional_params.name: algorithm
optional_params.description: Name of the segmentation algorithm to use. If None, uses the current analysis (optional)

outputs.description: Function that extracts segment data from a SegmentationPipe.

get_segment_data.description:
Get data for a specific segment

Parameters:
-----------
segment_id : Any
    ID of the segment to extract
algorithm : str, optional
    Name of the segmentation algorithm to use
    If None, uses the current analysis

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that extracts segment data from a SegmentationPipe

outputs.description: Function that compares segmentation algorithms in a SegmentationPipe.

compare_algorithms.description:
Compare all segmentation algorithms

Returns:
--------
Callable
    Function that compares segmentation algorithms in a SegmentationPipe

Apply a custom calculation or transformation to the dataframe

    A function that takes a DataFrame and returns a DataFrame
